00059   const int XMAX_MM = 320;
00065   const int YMAX_MM = 240;
00069   const int RESOLUTION = 96;
00073   static inline int ROUND_TO_LONG ( int n ) { return ((n+3)/4)*4; }

00081   struct WCHARSTR {
00082     WCHAR *const string_;       
00083     const int length_;          
00084 
00089     WCHARSTR ( WCHAR *const string, const int length )
00090       : string_( string ), length_( length ) {}
00091   };
00092 
00094 
00099   struct CHARSTR {
00100     CHAR *const string_;        
00101     const int length_;          
00102 
00107     CHARSTR ( CHAR *const string, const int length )
00108       : string_( string ), length_( length ) {}
00109   };
00110

00116   struct BYTEARRAY {
00117     BYTE *const array_;         
00118     const int n_;               
00119 
00124     BYTEARRAY ( BYTE *const array, const int n )
00125       : array_( array ), n_( n ) {}
00126   };
00127 
00129

00134     const DWORD n_;             
00135 
00140     POINTLARRAY ( POINTL *const points, const DWORD n )
00141       : points_( points ), n_( n ) {}
00142   };

00150     const DWORD n_;             
00151

00166     const DWORD n_;             
00167 
00172     INTARRAY ( INT *const ints, const DWORD n )
00173       : ints_( ints ), n_( n ) {}
00174   };
00175

00180   struct DWORDARRAY {
00181     DWORD *const dwords_;       
00182     const DWORD n_;             
00183 
00188     DWORDARRAY ( DWORD *const dwords, const DWORD n )
00189       : dwords_( dwords ), n_( n ) {}
00190   };

00196   struct PADDING {
00197     static const char padding_[4]; 
00198     const int size_;            
00199 
00203     PADDING ( const int size ) : size_( size ) {}
00204   };

00214   class DATASTREAM {
00215     bool swap_;
00216     ::FILE* fp_;
00217 
00218     static bool bigEndian ( void );
00219   public:
00225     DATASTREAM ( ::FILE* fp = 0 ) : swap_( bigEndian() ), fp_( fp ) {}
00230     void setStream ( ::FILE* fp ) { fp_ = fp; }
00235     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const BYTE& byte )
00236     {
00237       fwrite( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00238       return *this;
00239     }
00244     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( BYTE& byte )
00245     {
00246       fread( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00247       return *this;
00248     }
00253     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WORD& word )
00254     {
00255       if ( swap_ ) {
00256         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00257         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00258         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00259       }
00260       else
00261         fwrite( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00262       return *this;
00263     }
00268     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WORD& word )
00269     {
00270       if ( swap_ ) {
00271         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00272         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00273         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00274       }
00275       else
00276         fread( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00277       return *this;
00278     }
00283     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT16& word )
00284     {
00285       if ( swap_ ) {
00286         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00287         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00288         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00289       }
00290       else
00291         fwrite( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00292       return *this;
00293     }
00298     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT16& word )
00299     {
00300       if ( swap_ ) {
00301         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00302         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00303         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00304       }
00305       else
00306         fread( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00307       return *this;
00308     }
00313     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const DWORD& dword )
00314     {
00315       if ( swap_ ) {
00316         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&dword;
00317         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00318         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00319         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00320         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00321       }
00322       else
00323         fwrite( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00324       return *this;
00325     }
00330     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( DWORD& dword )
00331     {
00332       if ( swap_ ) {
00333         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&dword;
00334         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00335         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00336         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00337         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00338       }
00339       else
00340         fread( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00341       return *this;
00342     }
00343 #if !defined( __x86_64__ )
00344 
00348     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LONG& long_ )
00349     {
00350       if ( swap_ ) {
00351         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&long_;
00352         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00353         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00354         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00355         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00356       }
00357       else
00358         fwrite( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00359       return *this;
00360     }
00365     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LONG& long_ )
00366     {
00367       if ( swap_ ) {
00368         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&long_;
00369         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00370         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00371         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00372         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00373       }
00374       else
00375         fread( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00376       return *this;
00377     }
00378 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
00379 
00383     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT& int_ )
00384     {
00385       if ( swap_ ) {
00386         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&int_;
00387         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00388         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00389         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00390         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00391       }
00392       else
00393         fwrite( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00394       return *this;
00395     }
00400     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT& int_ )
00401     {
00402       if ( swap_ ) {
00403         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&int_;
00404         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00405         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00406         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00407         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00408       }
00409       else
00410         fread( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00411       return *this;
00412     }
00413 #if !defined(__x86_64__)
00414 
00418     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const UINT& uint )
00419     {
00420       if ( swap_ ) {
00421         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&uint;
00422         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00423         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00424         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00425         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00426       }
00427       else
00428         fwrite( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );
00429       return *this;
00430     }
00435     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( UINT& uint )
00436     {
00437       if ( swap_ ) {
00438         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&uint;
00439         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00440         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00441         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00442         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00443       }
00444       else
00445         fread( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );
00446       return *this;
00447     }
00448 #endif /* !__x86_64__ */
00449 
00453     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const FLOAT& float_ )
00454     {
00455       if ( swap_ ) {
00456         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&float_;
00457         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00458         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00459         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00460         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00461       }
00462       else
00463         fwrite( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00464       return *this;
00465     }
00470     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( FLOAT& float_ )
00471     {
00472       if ( swap_ ) {
00473         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&float_;
00474         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00475         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00476         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00477         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00478       }
00479       else
00480         fread( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00481       return *this;
00482     }
00487     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PADDING& padding )
00488     {
00489       if ( padding.size_ != 0 )
00490         fwrite( &padding.padding_, sizeof(CHAR), padding.size_, fp_ );
00491       return *this;
00492     }
00497     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const RECTL& rectl )
00498     {
00499       *this << rectl.left << rectl.top << rectl.right << rectl.bottom;
00500       return *this;
00501     }
00506     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( RECTL& rectl )
00507     {
00508       *this >> rectl.left >> rectl.top >> rectl.right >> rectl.bottom;
00509       return *this;
00510     }
00515     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const SIZEL& sizel )
00516     {
00517       *this << sizel.cx << sizel.cy;
00518       return *this;
00519     }
00524     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( SIZEL& sizel )
00525     {
00526       *this >> sizel.cx >> sizel.cy;
00527       return *this;
00528     }
00533     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00534     {
00535       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00536         *this << wcharstr.string_[i];
00537       return *this;
00538     }
00543     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00544     {
00545       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00546         *this >> wcharstr.string_[i];
00547       return *this;
00548     }
00553     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const CHARSTR& charstr )
00554     {
00555       fwrite( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00556       return *this;
00557     }
00562     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( CHARSTR& charstr )
00563     {
00564       fread( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00565       return *this;
00566     }
00571     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const ::EMR& emr )
00572     {
00573       *this << emr.iType << emr.nSize;
00574       return *this;
00575     }
00580     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( ::EMR& emr )
00581     {
00582       *this >> emr.iType >> emr.nSize;
00583       return *this;
00584     }
00589     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT& point )
00590     {
00591       *this << point.x << point.y;
00592       return *this;
00593     }
00598     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT& point )
00599     {
00600       *this >> point.x >> point.y;
00601       return *this;
00602     }
00607     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINTL& pointl )
00608     {
00609       *this << pointl.x << pointl.y;
00610       return *this;
00611     }
00616     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINTL& pointl )
00617     {
00618       *this >> pointl.x >> pointl.y;
00619       return *this;
00620     }
00625     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT16& point )
00626     {
00627       *this << point.x << point.y;
00628       return *this;
00629     }
00634     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT16& point )
00635     {
00636       *this >> point.x >> point.y;
00637       return *this;
00638     }
00643     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const XFORM& xform )
00644     {
00645       *this << xform.eM11 << xform.eM12 << xform.eM21 << xform.eM22
00646             << xform.eDx << xform.eDy;
00647       return *this;
00648     }
00653     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( XFORM& xform )
00654     {
00655       *this >> xform.eM11 >> xform.eM12 >> xform.eM21 >> xform.eM22
00656             >> xform.eDx >> xform.eDy;
00657       return *this;
00658     }
00663     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const BYTEARRAY& array )
00664     {
00665       fwrite( array.array_, sizeof(BYTE), array.n_, fp_ );
00666       return *this;
00667     }
00672     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( BYTEARRAY& array )
00673     {
00674       fread( array.array_, sizeof(BYTE), array.n_, fp_ );
00675       return *this;
00676     }
00681     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINTLARRAY& array )
00682     {
00683       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00684         *this << array.points_[i];
00685       return *this;
00686     }
00691     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINTLARRAY& array )
00692     {
00693       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00694         *this >> array.points_[i];
00695       return *this;
00696     }
00701     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT16ARRAY& array )
00702     {
00703       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00704         *this << array.points_[i];
00705       return *this;
00706     }
00711     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT16ARRAY& array )
00712     {
00713       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00714         *this >> array.points_[i];
00715       return *this;
00716     }
00721     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INTARRAY& array )
00722     {
00723       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00724         *this << array.ints_[i];
00725       return *this;
00726     }
00731     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INTARRAY& array )
00732     {
00733       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00734         *this >> array.ints_[i];
00735       return *this;
00736     }
00741     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const DWORDARRAY& array )
00742     {
00743       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00744         *this << array.dwords_[i];
00745       return *this;
00746     }
00751     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( DWORDARRAY& array )
00752     {
00753       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00754         *this >> array.dwords_[i];
00755       return *this;
00756     }
00761     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const ::EMRTEXT& text )
00762     {
00763       *this << text.ptlReference << text.nChars << text.offString << text.fOptions
00764          << text.rcl << text.offDx;
00765       return *this;
00766     }
00771     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( ::EMRTEXT& text )
00772     {
00773       *this >> text.ptlReference >> text.nChars >> text.offString >> text.fOptions
00774             >> text.rcl >> text.offDx;
00775       return *this;
00776     }
00781     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPEN& pen )
00782     {
00783       *this << pen.lopnStyle << pen.lopnWidth << pen.lopnColor;
00784       return *this;
00785     }
00790     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPEN& pen )
00791     {
00792       *this >> pen.lopnStyle >> pen.lopnWidth >> pen.lopnColor;
00793       return *this;
00794     }
00799     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00800     {
00801       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00802       *this << pen.elpPenStyle << pen.elpWidth << pen.elpBrushStyle << pen.elpColor
00803             << pen.elpHatch << pen.elpNumEntries;
00804       return *this;
00805     }
00810     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00811     {
00812       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00813       *this >> pen.elpPenStyle >> pen.elpWidth >> pen.elpBrushStyle >> pen.elpColor
00814             >> pen.elpHatch >> pen.elpNumEntries;
00815       return *this;
00816     }
00821     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGBRUSH& brush )
00822     {
00823       *this << brush.lbStyle << brush.lbColor << brush.lbHatch;
00824       return *this;
00825     }
00830     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGBRUSH& brush )
00831     {
00832       *this >> brush.lbStyle >> brush.lbColor >> brush.lbHatch;
00833       return *this;
00834     }
00839     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGFONTW& font )
00840     {
00841       *this << font.lfHeight << font.lfWidth << font.lfEscapement
00842             << font.lfOrientation << font.lfWeight << font.lfItalic
00843             << font.lfUnderline << font.lfStrikeOut << font.lfCharSet
00844             << font.lfOutPrecision << font.lfClipPrecision << font.lfQuality
00845             << font.lfPitchAndFamily
00846             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.lfFaceName), LF_FACESIZE );
00847       return *this;
00848     }
00853     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGFONTW& font )
00854     {
00855       WCHARSTR wFaceName( font.lfFaceName, LF_FACESIZE );
00856 
00857       *this >> font.lfHeight >> font.lfWidth >> font.lfEscapement
00858             >> font.lfOrientation >> font.lfWeight >> font.lfItalic
00859             >> font.lfUnderline >> font.lfStrikeOut >> font.lfCharSet
00860             >> font.lfOutPrecision >> font.lfClipPrecision >> font.lfQuality
00861             >> font.lfPitchAndFamily
00862             >> wFaceName;
00863       return *this;
00864     }
00869     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PANOSE& panose )
00870     {
00871       fwrite( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00872       return *this;
00873     }
00878     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( PANOSE& panose )
00879     {
00880       fread( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00881       return *this;
00882     }
00887     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00888     {
00889       *this << font.elfLogFont
00890             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfFullName),
00891                          LF_FULLFACESIZE )
00892             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfStyle), LF_FACESIZE )
00893             << font.elfVersion << font.elfStyleSize << font.elfMatch
00894             << font.elfReserved
00895             << BYTEARRAY( const_cast<BYTE*const>(font.elfVendorId),
00896                           ELF_VENDOR_SIZE )
00897             << font.elfCulture << font.elfPanose;
00898       return *this;
00899     }
00904     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00905     {
00906       WCHARSTR wFullName( font.elfFullName, LF_FULLFACESIZE );
00907       WCHARSTR wStyle( font.elfStyle, LF_FACESIZE );
00908       BYTEARRAY bVendorId( font.elfVendorId, ELF_VENDOR_SIZE );
00909       *this >> font.elfLogFont
00910             >> wFullName >> wStyle
00911             >> font.elfVersion >> font.elfStyleSize >> font.elfMatch
00912             >> font.elfReserved >> bVendorId
00913             >> font.elfCulture >> font.elfPanose;
00914       return *this;
00915     }
00920     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPALETTE& palette )
00921     {
00922       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00923       *this << palette.palVersion << palette.palNumEntries;
00924       return *this;
00925     }
00930     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPALETTE& palette )
00931     {
00932       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00933       *this >> palette.palVersion >> palette.palNumEntries;
00934       return *this;
00935     }
00936   };
00937 
00938   class METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT;
00939 
00941 
00947   class METARECORD {
00948   public:
00955     virtual void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const = 0;
00962     virtual bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds ) = 0;
00968     virtual int size ( void ) const = 0;
00974     virtual ~METARECORD( ) { }
00975 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00976 
00980     virtual void edit ( void ) const {}
00981 #endif
00982   };
00983 
00984 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00985   /* Miscellaneous editing routines */
00986   inline void edit_rectl ( const char* tag, const RECTL& rectl )
00987   {
00988 #if defined(__x86_64__)
00989     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%d, %d) - (%d, %d)\n";
00990 #else
00991     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%ld, %ld) - (%ld, %ld)\n";
00992 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
00993     printf( FMT, tag, rectl.left, rectl.top, rectl.right, rectl.bottom );
00994   }
00995 
00996   inline void edit_xform ( const char* tag, const XFORM& xform )
00997   {
00998     printf( "\t%s.eM11\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eM11 );
00999     printf( "\t%s.eM12\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eM12 );
01000     printf( "\t%s.eM21\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eM21 );
01001     printf( "\t%s.eM22\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eM22 );
01002     printf( "\t%s.eDx\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eDx );
01003     printf( "\t%s.eDy\t: %f\n", tag, xform.eDy );
01004   }
01005 
01006   inline void edit_color ( const char* tag, const COLORREF& color )
01007   {
01008 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01009     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: R(0x%02x) G(0x%02x) B(0x%02x)\n";
01010 #else
01011     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: R(0x%02lx) G(0x%02lx) B(0x%02lx)\n";
01012 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01013     printf( FMT, tag,
01014             GetRValue( color ), GetGValue( color ), GetBValue( color ) );
01015   }
01016 
01017   inline void edit_sizel ( const char* tag, const SIZEL& size )
01018   {
01019 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01020     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%d, %d)\n";
01021 #else
01022     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%ld, %ld)\n";
01023 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01024     printf( FMT, tag, size.cx, size.cy );
01025   }
01026 
01027   inline void edit_pointl ( const char* tag, const POINTL& point )
01028   {
01029 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01030     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%d, %d)\n";
01031 #else
01032     const char* FMT = "\t%s\t: (%ld, %ld)\n";
01033 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01034     printf( FMT, tag, point.x, point.y );
01035   }
01036 
01037   inline void edit_pointlarray ( const char* tag, const DWORD cptl,
01038                                   const POINTL* points )
01039   {
01040 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01041     const char* FMT0 = "\tcptl%s\t: %d\n";
01042     const char* FMT1 = "%d, %d\n";
01043     const char* FMT2 = "\t\t%s  %d, %d\n";
01044 #else
01045     const char* FMT0 = "\tcptl%s\t: %ld\n";
01046     const char* FMT1 = "%ld, %ld\n";
01047     const char* FMT2 = "\t\t%s  %ld, %ld\n";
01048 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01049     printf( FMT0, tag, cptl );
01050     printf( "\taptl%s\t: ", tag );
01051     if ( cptl > 0 )
01052       printf( FMT1, points[0].x, points[0].y );
01053     else
01054       puts( "" );
01055     for ( DWORD i = 1; i < cptl; i++ )
01056       printf( FMT2, tag, points[i].x, points[i].y );
01057   }
01058 
01059   inline void edit_point16array ( const char* tag, const unsigned int cpts,
01060                                   const POINT16* points )
01061   {
01062     printf( "\tcpts%s\t: %d\n", tag, cpts );
01063     printf( "\tapts%s\t: ", tag );
01064     if ( cpts > 0 )
01065       printf( "%d, %d\n", points[0].x, points[0].y );
01066     else
01067       puts( "" );
01068     for ( unsigned int i = 1; i < cpts; i++ )
01069       printf( "\t\t%s  %d, %d\n", tag, points[i].x, points[i].y );
01070   }
01071 
01072   inline void edit_pen_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
01073   {
01074     printf( "\t%s\t: ", tag );
01075     switch ( style & PS_STYLE_MASK ) {
01076     case PS_SOLID: printf( "PS_SOLID" ); break;
01077     case PS_DASH: printf( "PS_DASH" ); break;
01078     case PS_DOT: printf( "PS_DOT" ); break;
01079     case PS_DASHDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOT" ); break;
01080     case PS_DASHDOTDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOTDOT" ); break;
01081     case PS_NULL: printf( "PS_NULL" ); break;
01082     case PS_INSIDEFRAME: printf( "PS_INSIDEFRAME" ); break;
01083     case PS_USERSTYLE: printf( "PS_USERSTYLE" ); break;
01084     case PS_ALTERNATE: printf( "PS_ALTERNATE" ); break;
01085     }
01086     switch ( style & PS_ENDCAP_MASK ) {
01087     case PS_ENDCAP_ROUND: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_ROUND" ); break;
01088     case PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE" ); break;
01089     case PS_ENDCAP_FLAT: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_FLAT" ); break;
01090     }
01091     switch ( style & PS_JOIN_MASK ) {
01092     case PS_JOIN_ROUND: printf( " | PS_JOIN_ROUND" ); break;
01093     case PS_JOIN_BEVEL: printf( " | PS_JOIN_BEVEL" ); break;
01094     case PS_JOIN_MITER: printf( " | PS_JOIN_MITER" ); break;
01095     }
01096     switch ( style & PS_TYPE_MASK ) {
01097     case PS_COSMETIC: printf( " | PS_COSMETIC" ); break;
01098     case PS_GEOMETRIC: printf( " | PS_GEOMETRIC" ); break;
01099     }
01100     printf( "\n" );
01101   }
01102 
01103   inline void edit_brush_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
01104   {
01105 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01106     const char* FMT = "unknown(%d)";
01107 #else
01108     const char* FMT = "unknown(%ld)";
01109 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01110     printf( "\t%s\t: ", tag );
01111     switch ( style ) {
01112     case BS_SOLID: printf( "BS_SOLID" ); break;
01113     case BS_NULL: printf( "BS_NULL" ); break;
01114     case BS_HATCHED: printf( "BS_HATCHED" ); break;
01115     case BS_PATTERN: printf( "BS_PATTERN" ); break;
01116     case BS_INDEXED: printf( "BS_INDEXED" ); break;
01117     case BS_DIBPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN" ); break;
01118     case BS_DIBPATTERNPT: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERNPT" ); break;
01119     case BS_PATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_PATTERN8X8" ); break;
01120     case BS_DIBPATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
01121     case BS_MONOPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
01122     default: printf( FMT, style );
01123     }
01124     printf( "\n" );
01125   }
01126 
01127   inline void edit_brush_hatch ( const char* tag, DWORD hatch )
01128   {
01129 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01130     const char* FMT = "unknown(%d)";
01131 #else
01132     const char* FMT = "unknown(%ld)";
01133 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
01134     printf( "\t%s\t: ", tag );
01135     switch ( hatch ) {
01136     case HS_HORIZONTAL: printf( "HS_HORIZONTAL" ); break;
01137     case HS_VERTICAL: printf( "HS_VERTICAL" ); break;
01138     case HS_FDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_FDIAGONAL" ); break;
01139     case HS_BDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_BDIAGONAL" ); break;
01140     case HS_CROSS: printf( "HS_CROSS" ); break;
01141     case HS_DIAGCROSS: printf( "HS_DIAGCROSS" ); break;
01142     default: printf( FMT, hatch );
01143     }
01144     printf( "\n" );
01145   }
01146 #endif
01147 
01154   enum OBJECTTYPE { O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT     = OBJ_METADC,
01155                     O_FONT                      = OBJ_FONT,
01156                     O_PEN                       = OBJ_PEN,
01157                     O_EXTPEN                    = OBJ_EXTPEN,
01158                     O_BRUSH                     = OBJ_BRUSH,
01159                     O_PALETTE                   = OBJ_PAL };
01160 #if 0
01161 
01164   static char* typStr ( OBJECTTYPE type )
01165   {
01166     switch (type) {
01167     case O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT:
01168       return "metafile device context";
01169     case O_FONT:
01170       return "font";
01171     case O_PEN:
01172       return "pen";
01173     case O_EXTPEN:
01174       return "extended pen";
01175     case O_BRUSH:
01176       return "brush";
01177     case O_PALETTE:
01178       return "palette";
01179     }
01180     return "unknown object";
01181   }
01182 #endif
01184 
01189   class OBJECT {
01190   public:
01191     HGDIOBJ handle;             
01192 
01193     virtual ~OBJECT () {}
01198     OBJECT ( void ) : handle( 0 ) {}
01202     virtual OBJECTTYPE getType ( void ) const = 0;
01203   };
01204 
01206 
01211   class GRAPHICSOBJECT : public OBJECT {
01212   public:
01214     virtual ~GRAPHICSOBJECT () {}
01219     std::map< HDC, HGDIOBJ > contexts;
01225     virtual METARECORD* newEMR ( HDC dc, HGDIOBJ handle ) = 0;
01226   };
01227 
01228   typedef METARECORD*(*METARECORDCTOR)(DATASTREAM&);
01229 
01230   class GLOBALOBJECTS {
01234     std::vector<OBJECT*> objects;
01235 
01242     std::map< DWORD, METARECORDCTOR > new_records;
01243 
01244   public:
01245     GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
01246     ~GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
01247     HGDIOBJ add ( OBJECT* object );
01248     OBJECT* find ( const HGDIOBJ handle );
01249     void remove ( const OBJECT* object );
01250 
01251     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator begin ( void ) const
01252     { return objects.begin(); }
01253     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator end ( void ) const
01254     { return objects.end(); }
01255 
01256     METARECORDCTOR newRecord ( DWORD iType ) const;
01257 
01258     static EMF::METARECORD* new_eof ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01259     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportorgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01260     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindoworgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01261     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01262     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01263     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scaleviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01264     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scalewindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01265     static EMF::METARECORD* new_modifyworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01266     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01267     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextalign ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01268     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01269     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01270     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01271     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpolyfillmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01272     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setmapmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01273     static EMF::METARECORD* new_selectobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01274     static EMF::METARECORD* new_deleteobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01275     static EMF::METARECORD* new_movetoex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01276     static EMF::METARECORD* new_lineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01277     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01278     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arcto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01279     static EMF::METARECORD* new_rectangle ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01280     static EMF::METARECORD* new_ellipse ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01281     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01282     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01283     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01284     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polygon16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01285     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polypolygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01286     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polypolygon16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01287     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01288     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01289     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01290     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01291     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01292     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01293     static EMF::METARECORD* new_exttextouta ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01294     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpixelv ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01295     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createpen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01296     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatepen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01297     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createbrushindirect ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01298     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatefontindirectw ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01299     static EMF::METARECORD* new_fillpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01300     static EMF::METARECORD* new_strokepath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01301     static EMF::METARECORD* new_strokeandfillpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01302     static EMF::METARECORD* new_beginpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01303     static EMF::METARECORD* new_endpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01304     static EMF::METARECORD* new_closefigure ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01305     static EMF::METARECORD* new_savedc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01306     static EMF::METARECORD* new_restoredc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01307     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setmetargn ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01308   };

01310   extern GLOBALOBJECTS globalObjects;
01311 
01313 
01319   class ENHMETAHEADER : public METARECORD, public ::ENHMETAHEADER {
01320 
01321     LPWSTR description_w;
01322     int description_size;
01323 
01324   public:
01331     ENHMETAHEADER ( LPCWSTR description = 0 )
01332       : description_w( 0 ), description_size( 0 )
01333     {
01334       iType = EMR_HEADER;
01335       nSize = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01336 
01337       // Compute the bounds
01338       RECTL default_bounds = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01339       rclBounds = default_bounds;
01340       RECTL default_frame = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01341       rclFrame = default_frame;
01342       dSignature = ENHMETA_SIGNATURE;
01343       nVersion = 0x10000;
01344       nBytes = nSize;
01345       nRecords = 1;
01346       nHandles = 0;
01347       sReserved = 0;
01348       nDescription = 0;
01349       offDescription = 0;
01350       nPalEntries = 0;
01351       szlDevice.cx = XMAX_PIXELS;
01352       szlDevice.cy = YMAX_PIXELS;
01353       szlMillimeters.cx = XMAX_MM;
01354       szlMillimeters.cy = YMAX_MM;
01355       //
01356       cbPixelFormat = 0;
01357       offPixelFormat = 0;
01358       bOpenGL = FALSE;
01359       //
01360 #if 1
01361       szlMicrometers.cx = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cx;
01362       szlMicrometers.cy = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cy;
01363 #endif
01364       if ( description ) {
01365         // Count the number of characters in the description
01366         int description_count = 0, nulls = 0;
01367         LPCWSTR description_p = description;
01368         while ( nulls < 3 ) {
01369           description_count++;
01370           if ( (*description_p++) == 0 ) nulls++;
01371         }
01372 
01373         // Make sure that the TOTAL record length will be a multiple of 4
01374 
01375         int record_size = ROUND_TO_LONG( sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER ) +
01376                                          sizeof( WCHAR ) * description_count );
01377         description_size =
01378           (record_size - sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER )) / sizeof( WCHAR );
01379 
01380         description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01381 
01382         memset( description_w, 0, sizeof(WCHAR) * description_size );
01383 
01384         for ( int i=0; i<description_count; i++ )
01385           description_w[i] = *description++;
01386 
01387         nSize = nBytes = record_size;
01388         nDescription = description_count;
01389         offDescription = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01390       }
01391     }
01392     
01396     ~ENHMETAHEADER ( )
01397     {
01398       if ( description_w ) delete[] description_w;
01399     }
01404     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01405     {
01406       ds << iType << nSize
01407          << rclBounds << rclFrame
01408          << dSignature << nVersion << nBytes << nRecords << nHandles << sReserved
01409          << nDescription << offDescription << nPalEntries
01410          << szlDevice << szlMillimeters
01411          << cbPixelFormat << offPixelFormat << bOpenGL
01412 #if 1
01413          << szlMicrometers
01414 #endif
01415          << WCHARSTR( description_w, description_size );
01416       return true;
01417     }
01421     bool unserialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01422     {
01423       ds >> iType >> nSize
01424          >> rclBounds >> rclFrame
01425          >> dSignature >> nVersion >> nBytes >> nRecords >> nHandles >> sReserved
01426          >> nDescription >> offDescription >> nPalEntries
01427          >> szlDevice >> szlMillimeters;
01428 
01429       // Some elements of the metafile header were added at later dates
01430 
01431 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((char*)&(((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01432 (char*)((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01433 #if 1
01434       if ( OffsetOf( this, szlMicrometers ) <= offDescription )
01435         ds >> cbPixelFormat >> offPixelFormat >> bOpenGL;
01436 #else
01437       if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription )
01438         ds >> cbPixelFormat >> offPixelFormat >> bOpenGL;
01439 #endif
01440 #undef OffsetOf
01441 #if 1
01442       if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription )
01443         ds >> szlMicrometers;
01444 #endif
01445       // Should now probably check that the offset is correct...
01446 
01447       description_size = ( nSize - offDescription ) / sizeof(WCHAR);
01448       description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01449 
01450       WCHARSTR description( description_w, description_size );
01451 
01452       ds >> description;
01453 
01454       return true;
01455     }
01459     int size ( void ) const { return nSize; }
01465     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC /*dc*/ ) const
01466     {
01467       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01468     }
01469 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01470 
01473     void edit ( void ) const
01474     {
01475 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01476       const char* FMT0 = "\tiType\t\t\t: %d\n";
01477       const char* FMT1 = "\tnSize\t\t\t: %d\n";
01478       const char* FMT2 = "\tnBytes\t\t\t: %d\n";
01479       const char* FMT3 = "\tnRecords\t\t: %d\n";
01480       const char* FMT4 = "\tnDescription\t\t: %d\n";
01481       const char* FMT5 = "\toffDescription\t\t: %d\n";
01482       const char* FMT6 = "\tnPalEntries\t\t: %d\n";
01483       const char* FMT7 = "\tcbPixelFormat\t\t: %d\n";
01484       const char* FMT8 = "\toffPixelFormat\t\t: %d\n";
01485       const char* FMT9 = "\tbOpenGL\t\t\t: %d\n";
01486 #else
01487       const char* FMT0 = "\tiType\t\t\t: %ld\n";
01488       const char* FMT1 = "\tnSize\t\t\t: %ld\n";
01489       const char* FMT2 = "\tnBytes\t\t\t: %ld\n";
01490       const char* FMT3 = "\tnRecords\t\t: %ld\n";
01491       const char* FMT4 = "\tnDescription\t\t: %ld\n";
01492       const char* FMT5 = "\toffDescription\t\t: %ld\n";
01493       const char* FMT6 = "\tnPalEntries\t\t: %ld\n";
01494       const char* FMT7 = "\tcbPixelFormat\t\t: %ld\n";
01495       const char* FMT8 = "\toffPixelFormat\t\t: %ld\n";
01496       const char* FMT9 = "\tbOpenGL\t\t\t: %ld\n";
01497 #endif
01498       printf( "*HEADER*\n" );
01499       printf( FMT0, iType );
01500       printf( FMT1, nSize );
01501       edit_rectl( "rclBounds\t", rclBounds );
01502       edit_rectl( "rclFrame\t", rclFrame );
01503       printf( "\tdSignature\t\t: %.4s\n", (const char*)&dSignature );
01504       printf( "\tnVersion\t\t: 0x%x\n", (unsigned int)nVersion );
01505       printf( FMT2, nBytes );
01506       printf( FMT3, nRecords );
01507       printf( "\tnHandles\t\t: %d\n", nHandles );
01508       printf( FMT4, nDescription );
01509       printf( FMT5, offDescription );
01510       printf( FMT6, nPalEntries );
01511       edit_sizel( "szlDevice\t", szlDevice );
01512       edit_sizel( "szlMillimeters\t", szlMillimeters );
01513 
01514       /* Make a crude guess as to the age of this file */
01515 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((const char*)&(((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01516 (const char*)((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01517 
01518       if ( OffsetOf( this, cbPixelFormat ) <= offDescription ) {
01519         printf( FMT7, cbPixelFormat );
01520         printf( FMT8, offPixelFormat );
01521         printf( FMT9, bOpenGL );
01522 #if 1
01523         if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription ) {
01524           edit_sizel( "szlMicrometers\t", szlMicrometers );
01525         }
01526 #endif
01527       }
01528 
01529 #undef OffsetOf
01530 
01531       if ( nDescription != 0 ) {
01532 
01533         wchar_t last_w = 0;
01534         WCHAR* description = description_w;
01535 
01536         printf( "\tDescription:" );
01537 
01538         for ( DWORD i = 0; i < nDescription; i++ ) {
01539 
01540           wchar_t w = *description++; /* This is not true, really. UNICODE is not
01541                                        * glibc's wide character representation */
01542 
01543           if ( w != 0 ) {
01544             if ( last_w == 0 ) printf( "\n\t\t" );
01545             putchar( w );
01546           }
01547           
01548           last_w = w;
01549         }
01550         printf( "\n" );
01551       }
01552     }
01553 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01554   };
01555 
01557 
01562   class EMREOF : public METARECORD, ::EMREOF {
01563   public:
01567     EMREOF ( void )
01568     {
01569       emr.iType = EMR_EOF;
01570       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMREOF );
01571       nPalEntries = 0;
01572       offPalEntries = 0;
01573       nSizeLast = 0;
01574     }
01575 
01580     EMREOF ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01581     {
01582       ds >> emr >> nPalEntries >> offPalEntries >> nSizeLast;
01583     }
01584 
01588     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01589     {
01590       ds << emr << nPalEntries << offPalEntries << nSizeLast;
01591       return true;
01592     }
01596     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01602     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC /*dc*/ ) const
01603     {
01604       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01605     }
01606 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01607 
01610     void edit ( void ) const
01611     {
01612       printf( "*EOF*\n" );
01613     }
01614 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01615   };
01616 
01618 
01623   class EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX {
01624   public:
01629     EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX ( INT x, INT y )

01827     {
01828       ds >> emr >> xNum >> xDenom >> yNum >> yDenom;
01829     }
01833     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )

01850     }

01852 
01855     void edit ( void ) const
01856     {
01857 #if defined(__x86_64__)
01858       const char* FMT0 = "\txNum\t: %d\n";
01859       const char* FMT1 = "\txDenom\t: %d\n";
01860       const char* FMT2 = "\tyNum\t: %d\n";
01861       const char* FMT3 = "\tyDenom\t: %d\n";
01862 #else
01863       const char* FMT0 = "\txNum\t: %ld\n";
01864       const char* FMT1 = "\txDenom\t: %ld\n";
01865       const char* FMT2 = "\tyNum\t: %ld\n";
01866       const char* FMT3 = "\tyDenom\t: %ld\n";
01867 #endif
01868       printf( "*SCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX*\n" );
01869       printf( FMT0, xNum );
01870       printf( FMT1, xDenom );
01871       printf( FMT2, yNum );

01873     }
01874 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01875   };

02022   class EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM : public METARECORD, ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM {
02023   public:
02029     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( const XFORM* transform, DWORD mode )
02030     {
02031       emr.iType = EMR_MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM;
02032       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM );
02033       xform = *transform;
02034       iMode = mode;
02035     }
02040     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02041     {
02042       ds >> emr >> xform >> iMode;
02043     }
02047     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02048     {
02049       ds << emr << xform << iMode;
02050       return true;
02051     }
02055     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02061     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02062     {
02063       ModifyWorldTransform( dc, &xform, iMode );
02064     }
02065 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02066 
02069     void edit ( void ) const
02070     {
02071 #if defined(__x86_64__)
02072     const char* FMT = "unknown(%d)\n";
02073 #else
02074     const char* FMT = "unknown(%ld)\n";
02075 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
02076       printf( "*MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM*\n" );
02077       edit_xform( "xform", xform );
02078       printf( "\tiMode\t\t: " );
02079       switch ( iMode ) {
02080       case MWT_IDENTITY: printf( "MWT_IDENTITY\n" ); break;
02081       case MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
02082       case MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
02083       default: printf( FMT, iMode );
02084       }
02085     }
02086 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02087   };
02088

02152   class EMRSETTEXTALIGN : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN {
02153   public:
02157     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( UINT mode )
02158     {
02159       emr.iType = EMR_SETTEXTALIGN;
02160       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN );
02161       iMode = mode;
02162     }
02167     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02168     {
02169       ds >> emr >> iMode;
02170     }
02174     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02175     {
02176       ds << emr << iMode;
02177       return true;
02178     }
02182     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02188     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02189     {
02190       SetTextAlign( dc, iMode );
02191     }
02192 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02193 
02196     void edit ( void ) const
02197     {
02198 #if defined(__x86_64__)
02199     const char* FMT = "| unknown bits(0x%x)";
02200 #else
02201     const char* FMT = "| unknown bits(0x%lx)";
02202 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
02203       unsigned int known_bits = TA_BASELINE+TA_CENTER+TA_UPDATECP+TA_RTLREADING;
02204       unsigned int unknown_bits = ~known_bits;
02205 
02206       printf( "*SETTEXTALIGN*\n" );
02207       printf( "\tiMode\t: " );
02208       if ( iMode & TA_UPDATECP )
02209         printf( "TA_UPDATECP" );
02210       else
02211         printf( "TA_NOUPDATECP" );
02212       if ( iMode & TA_CENTER )
02213         printf( " | TA_CENTER" );
02214       else if ( iMode & TA_RIGHT )
02215         printf( " | TA_RIGHT" );
02216       else
02217         printf( " | TA_LEFT" );
02218       if ( iMode & TA_BASELINE )
02219         printf( " | TA_BASELINE" );
02220       else if ( iMode & TA_BOTTOM )
02221         printf( " | TA_BOTTOM" );
02222       else
02223         printf( " | TA_TOP" );
02224       if ( iMode & TA_RTLREADING )
02225         printf( " | TA_RTLREADING" );
02226       if ( iMode & unknown_bits )
02227         printf( FMT, iMode & unknown_bits );
02228       printf( "\n" );
02229     }
02230 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02231   };
02232 
02234

02658 #else
02659       const char* FMT = "\tihObject\t: 0x%lx\n";
02660 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
02661       printf( "*DELETEOBJECT*\n" );
02662       printf( FMT, ihObject );
02663     }
02664 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02665   };
02666 
02668 
02671   class EMRMOVETOEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRMOVETOEX {
02672   public:
02677     EMRMOVETOEX ( INT x, INT y )
02678     {
02679       emr.iType = EMR_MOVETOEX;
02680       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRMOVETOEX );
02681       ptl.x = x;
02682       ptl.y = y;
02683     }
02688     EMRMOVETOEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02689     {
02690       ds >> emr >> ptl;
02691     }
02695     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02696     {
02697       ds << emr << ptl;
02698       return true;
02699     }
02703     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02709     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02710     {
02711       MoveToEx( dc,  ptl.x, ptl.y, 0 );
02712     }
02713 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02714 
02717     void edit ( void ) const
02718     {
02719       printf( "*MOVETOEX*\n" );
02720       edit_pointl( "ptl", ptl );
02721     }
02722 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02723   };

02787   class EMRARC : public METARECORD, ::EMRARC {
02788   public:
02800     EMRARC ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom, INT xstart,
02801              INT ystart, INT xend, INT yend )
02802     {
02803       emr.iType = EMR_ARC;
02804       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRARC );
02805       rclBox.left = left;
02806       rclBox.right = right;
02807       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02808       rclBox.top = top;
02809       ptlStart.x = xstart;
02810       ptlStart.y = ystart;
02811       ptlEnd.x = xend;
02812       ptlEnd.y = yend;
02813     }
02818     EMRARC ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02819     {
02820       ds >> emr >> rclBox >> ptlStart >> ptlEnd;
02821     }
02825     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02826     {
02827       ds << emr << rclBox << ptlStart << ptlEnd;
02828       return true;
02829     }
02833     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02839     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02840     {
02841       Arc( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom,
02842            ptlStart.x, ptlStart.y, ptlEnd.x, ptlEnd.y );
02843     }
02844 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02845 
02848     void edit ( void ) const
02849     {
02850       printf( "*ARC*\n" );
02851       edit_rectl( "rclBox\t", rclBox );
02852       edit_pointl( "ptlStart", ptlStart );
02853       edit_pointl( "ptlEnd\t", ptlEnd );
02854     }
02855 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02856   };

03059 
03062   class EMRPOLYLINE : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE {
03063     POINTL* lpoints;
03064   public:
03070     EMRPOLYLINE ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03071     {
03072       cptl = n;
03073       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03074       aptl[0].y = 0;
03075 
03076       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE;
03077       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03078       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * ( cptl - 1);
03079 
03080       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03081 
03082       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03083         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03084         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03085       }
03086 
03087       rclBounds = *bounds;
03088     }
03092     ~EMRPOLYLINE ( )
03093     {
03094       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03095     }
03100     EMRPOLYLINE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03101     {
03102       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03103 
03104       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03105 
03106       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03107 
03108       ds >> points;
03109     }
03113     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03114     {
03115       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03116       return true;
03117     }
03121     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03127     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03128     {
03129       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03130       Polyline( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03131     }
03132 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03133 
03136     void edit ( void ) const
03137     {
03138       printf( "*POLYLINE*\n" );
03139       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03140 #if 0
03141       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03142       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03143       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03144         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03145 #else
03146       edit_pointlarray( "\t", cptl, lpoints );
03147 #endif
03148     }
03149 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03150   };
03151 
03153 
03156   class EMRPOLYLINE16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE16 {
03157     POINT16* lpoints;
03158   public:
03164     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points, INT n )
03165     {
03166       cpts = n;
03167       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03168       apts[0].y = 0;
03169 
03170       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE16;
03171       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03172       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * ( cpts - 1);
03173 
03174       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03175 
03176       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03177         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03178         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03179       }
03180 
03181       rclBounds = *bounds;
03182     }
03189     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03190     {
03191       cpts = n;
03192       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03193       apts[0].y = 0;
03194 
03195       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE16;
03196       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03197       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * ( cpts - 1);
03198 
03199       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03200 
03201       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03202         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03203         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03204       }
03205 
03206       rclBounds = *bounds;
03207     }
03211     ~EMRPOLYLINE16 ( )
03212     {
03213       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03214     }
03219     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03220     {
03221       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cpts;
03222 
03223       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03224 
03225       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03226 
03227       ds >> points;
03228     }
03232     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03233     {
03234       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03235       return true;
03236     }
03240     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03246     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03247     {
03248       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03249       Polyline16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03250     }
03251 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03252 
03255     void edit ( void ) const
03256     {
03257       printf( "*POLYLINE16*\n" );
03258       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03259       edit_point16array( "\t", cpts, lpoints );
03260     }
03261 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03262   };
03263 
03265 
03268   class EMRPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYGON {
03269     POINTL* lpoints;
03270   public:
03276     EMRPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03277     {
03278       cptl = n;
03279       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03280       aptl[0].y = 0;
03281 
03282       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON;
03283       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03284       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
03285 
03286       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03287 
03288       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03289         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03290         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03291       }
03292 
03293       rclBounds = *bounds;
03294     }
03299     EMRPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03300     {
03301       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03302 
03303       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03304 
03305       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03306 
03307       ds >> points;
03308     }
03312     ~EMRPOLYGON ( )
03313     {
03314       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03315     }
03319     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03320     {
03321       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03322       return true;
03323     }
03327     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03333     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03334     {
03335       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03336       Polygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03337     }
03338 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03339 
03342     void edit ( void ) const
03343     {
03344       printf( "*POLYGON*\n" );
03345       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03346 #if 0
03347       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03348       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03349       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03350         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03351 #else
03352       edit_pointlarray( "\t", cptl, lpoints );
03353 #endif
03354     }
03355 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03356   };

03362   class EMRPOLYGON16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYGON16 {
03363     POINT16* lpoints;
03364   public:
03370     EMRPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT16 n )
03371     {
03372       cpts = n;
03373       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03374       apts[0].y = 0;
03375 
03376       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON16;
03377       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03378       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1);
03379 
03380       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03381 
03382       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03383         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03384         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03385       }
03386 
03387       rclBounds = *bounds;
03388     }
03395     EMRPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points, INT16 n )
03396     {
03397       cpts = n;
03398       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03399       apts[0].y = 0;
03400 
03401       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON16;
03402       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03403       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1);
03404 
03405       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03406 
03407       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03408         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03409         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03410       }
03411 
03412       rclBounds = *bounds;
03413     }
03418     EMRPOLYGON16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03419     {
03420       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cpts;
03421 
03422       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03423 
03424       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03425 
03426       ds >> points;
03427     }
03431     ~EMRPOLYGON16 ( )
03432     {
03433       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03434     }
03438     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03439     {
03440       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03441       return true;
03442     }
03446     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03452     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03453     {
03454       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03455       Polygon16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03456     }
03457 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03458 
03461     void edit ( void ) const
03462     {
03463       printf( "*POLYGON16*\n" );
03464       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03465       edit_point16array( "\t", cpts, lpoints );
03466     }
03467 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03468   };
03469 
03471 
03474   class EMRPOLYPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON {
03475     DWORD* lcounts;
03476     POINTL* lpoints;
03477   public:
03484     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, const INT* counts,
03485                      UINT polygons )
03486     {
03487       nPolys = polygons;
03488       // Count the number of points in points
03489       int n = 0;
03490       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03491         n += counts[i];
03492 
03493       cptl = n;
03494       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03495       aptl[0].x = 0;
03496       aptl[0].y = 0;
03497 
03498       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON;
03499       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03500       // aren't directly written out
03501       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1)
03502         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03503 
03504       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03505 
03506       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03507         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03508 
03509       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03510 
03511       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03512         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03513         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03514       }
03515 
03516       rclBounds = *bounds;
03517     }
03521     ~EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( )
03522     {
03523       if ( lcounts ) delete[] lcounts;
03524       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03525     }
03530     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03531     {
03532       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> nPolys >> cptl;
03533 
03534       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03535 
03536       DWORDARRAY counts( lcounts, nPolys );
03537 
03538       ds >> counts;
03539 
03540       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03541 
03542       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03543 
03544       ds >> points;
03545     }
03549     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03550     {
03551       ds << emr << rclBounds << nPolys << cptl << DWORDARRAY( lcounts, nPolys )
03552          << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03553       return true;
03554     }
03558     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03564     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03565     {
03566       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03567       // (but DWORD and INT are not)
03568       INT* counts = new INT[nPolys];
03569       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03570         counts[i] = lcounts[i];
03571 
03572       PolyPolygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, counts, nPolys );
03573 
03574       delete[] counts;
03575     }
03576 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03577 
03580     void edit ( void ) const
03581     {
03582 #if defined(__x86_64__)
03583       const char* FMT0 = "\tnPolys\t\t: %d\n";
03584       const char* FMT1 = "\tcptl\t\t: %d\n";
03585       const char* FMT2 = "%d\n";
03586       const char* FMT3 = "\t\t\t  %d\n";
03587       const char* FMT4 = "%d, %d\n";
03588       const char* FMT5 = "\t\t\t  %d, %d\n";
03589 #else
03590       const char* FMT0 = "\tnPolys\t\t: %ld\n";
03591       const char* FMT1 = "\tcptl\t\t: %ld\n";
03592       const char* FMT2 = "%ld\n";
03593       const char* FMT3 = "\t\t\t  %ld\n";
03594       const char* FMT4 = "%ld, %ld\n";
03595       const char* FMT5 = "\t\t\t  %ld, %ld\n";
03596 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
03597       printf( "*POLYPOLYGON*\n" );
03598       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03599       printf( FMT0, nPolys );
03600       printf( FMT1, cptl );
03601       printf( "\taPolyCounts\t: " );
03602       if ( nPolys > 0 )
03603         printf( FMT2, lcounts[0] );
03604       else
03605         puts( "" );
03606       for ( unsigned int i = 1; i < nPolys; i++ )
03607         printf( FMT3, lcounts[i] );
03608       printf( "\tapts\t\t: " );
03609       if ( cptl > 0 )
03610         printf( FMT4, lpoints[0].x, lpoints[0].y );
03611       else
03612         puts( "" );
03613       for ( unsigned int i = 1; i < cptl; i++ )
03614         printf( FMT5, lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03615     }
03616 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03617   };
03618 
03620 
03623   class EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 {
03624     DWORD* lcounts;
03625     POINT16* lpoints;
03626   public:
03633     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points,
03634                        const INT* counts, UINT polygons )
03635     {
03636       nPolys = polygons;
03637       // Count the number of points in points
03638       int n = 0;
03639       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03640         n += counts[i];
03641 
03642       cpts = n;
03643       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03644       apts[0].x = 0;
03645       apts[0].y = 0;
03646 
03647       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON16;
03648       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03649       // aren't directly written out
03650       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1)
03651         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03652 
03653       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03654 
03655       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03656         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03657 
03658       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03659 
03660       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03661         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03662         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03663       }
03664 
03665       rclBounds = *bounds;
03666     }
03674     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points,
03675                        const INT* counts, UINT16 polygons )
03676     {
03677       nPolys = polygons;
03678       // Count the number of points in points
03679       int n = 0;
03680       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03681         n += counts[i];
03682 
03683       cpts = n;
03684       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03685       apts[0].x = 0;
03686       apts[0].y = 0;
03687 
03688       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON16;
03689       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03690       // aren't directly written out
03691       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1)
03692         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03693 
03694       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03695 
03696       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03697         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03698 
03699       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03700 
03701       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03702         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03703         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03704       }
03705 
03706       rclBounds = *bounds;
03707     }
03711     ~EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( )
03712     {
03713       if ( lcounts ) delete[] lcounts;
03714       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03715     }
03720     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03721     {
03722       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> nPolys >> cpts;
03723 
03724       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03725 
03726       DWORDARRAY counts( lcounts, nPolys );
03727 
03728       ds >> counts;
03729 
03730       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03731 
03732       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03733 
03734       ds >> points;
03735     }
03739     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03740     {
03741       ds << emr << rclBounds << nPolys << cpts << DWORDARRAY( lcounts, nPolys )
03742          << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03743       return true;
03744     }
03748     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03754     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03755     {
03756       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03757       // (but DWORD and INT are not)
03758       INT* counts = new INT[nPolys];
03759       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03760         counts[i] = lcounts[i];
03761 
03762       PolyPolygon16( dc, lpoints, counts, nPolys );
03763 
03764       delete[] counts;
03765     }
03766 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03767 
03770     void edit ( void ) const
03771     {
03772 #if defined(__x86_64__)
03773       const char* FMT0 = "\tnPolys\t\t: %d\n";
03774       const char* FMT1 = "\tcptl\t\t: %d\n";
03775       const char* FMT2 = "%d\n";
03776       const char* FMT3 = "\t\t\t  %d\n";
03777 #else
03778       const char* FMT0 = "\tnPolys\t\t: %ld\n";
03779       const char* FMT1 = "\tcptl\t\t: %ld\n";
03780       const char* FMT2 = "%ld\n";
03781       const char* FMT3 = "\t\t\t  %ld\n";
03782 #endif /* __x86_64__ */
03783       printf( "*POLYPOLYGON16*\n" );
03784       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03785       printf( FMT0, nPolys );
03786       printf( FMT1, cpts );
03787       printf( "\taPolyCounts\t: " );
03788       if ( nPolys > 0 )
03789         printf( FMT2, lcounts[0] );
03790       else
03791         puts( "" );
03792       for ( unsigned int i = 1; i < nPolys; i++ )
03793         printf( FMT3, lcounts[i] );
03794       printf( "\tapts\t\t: " );
03795       if ( cpts > 0 )
03796         printf( "%d, %d\n", lpoints[0].x, lpoints[0].y );
03797       else
03798         puts( "" );
03799       for ( unsigned int i = 1; i < cpts; i++ )
03800         printf( "\t\t\t  %d, %d\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03801     }
03802 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03803   };
03804 
03806 
03809   class EMRPOLYBEZIER : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYBEZIER {
03810     POINTL* lpoints;
03811   public:
03817     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03818     {
03819       cptl = n;
03820       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03821       aptl[0].y = 0;
03822 
03823       emr.iType = EMR_POLYBEZIER;
03824       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03825       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYBEZIER ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
03826 
03827       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03828 
03829       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03830         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03831         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03832       }
03833 
03834       rclBounds = *bounds;
03835     }
03840     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03841     {
03842       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03843 
03844       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03845 
03846       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03847 
03848       ds >> points;
03849     }
03853     ~EMRPOLYBEZIER ( )
03854     {
03855       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03856     }
03860     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03861     {
03862       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03863       return true;
03864     }
03868     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03874     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03875     {
03876       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03877       PolyBezier( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03878     }
03879 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03880 
03883     void edit ( void ) const
03884     {
03885       printf( "*POLYBEZIER*\n" );
03886       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03887 #if 0
03888       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03889       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03890       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03891         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03892 #else
03893       edit_pointlarray( "\t", cptl, lpoints );
03894 #endif
03895     }
03896 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03897   };
03898 
03900

03925         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;

03937     {

03939       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03940       apts[0].y = 0;
03941

03945

03947 
03948       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {

03950         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;

03953       rclBounds = *bounds;
03954     }
03959     EMRPOLYBEZIER16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03960     {

03962 
03963       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03964 
03965       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03966

03972     ~EMRPOLYBEZIER16 ( )

03980     {

03982       return true;
03983     }
03987     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03993     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03994     {
03995       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03996       PolyBezier16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03997     }
03998 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03999 
04002     void edit ( void ) const
04003     {
04004       printf( "*POLYBEZIER16*\n" );
04005       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
04006       edit_point16array( "\t", cpts, lpoints );
04007     }
04008 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
04009   };

04015   class EMRPOLYBEZIERTO : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYBEZIER {
04016     POINTL* lpoints;
04017   public:
04023     EMRPOLYBEZIERTO ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
04024     {
04025       cptl = n;
04026       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
04027       aptl[0].y = 0;
04028

04030       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out

04032 
04033       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
04034 
04035       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {

04037         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
04038       }
04039

04041     }
04046     EMRPOLYBEZIERTO ( DATASTREAM& ds )
04047     {
04048       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
04049

04080     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
04081     {
04082       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
04083       PolyBezierTo( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
04084     }
04085 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
04086

04090     {
04091       printf( "*POLYBEZIERTO*\n" );
04092       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
04093 #if 0
04094       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
04095       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
04096       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
04097         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
04098 #else
04099       edit_pointlarray( "\t", cptl, lpoints );
04100 #endif
04101     }
04102 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
04103   };

04221   class EMRPOLYLINETO : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINETO {
04222     POINTL* lpoints;
04223   public:
04229     EMRPOLYLINETO ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
04230     {
04231       cptl = n;
04232       aptl[0].x = 0;
04233       aptl[0].y = 0;
04234 
04235       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINETO;
04236       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
04237       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINETO ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
04238 
04239       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
04240 
04241       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
04242         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
04243         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
04244       }
04245 
04246       rclBounds = *bounds;
04247     }
04252     EMRPOLYLINETO ( DATASTREAM& ds )
04253     {
04254       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
04255 
04256       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
04257 
04258       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
04259 
04260       ds >> points;
04261     }
04265     ~EMRPOLYLINETO ( )
04266     {
04267       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
04268     }
04272     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
04273     {
04274       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
04275       return true;
04276     }
04280     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
04286     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
04287     {
04288       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
04289       PolylineTo( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
04290     }
04291 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
04292 
04295     void edit ( void ) const
04296     {
04297       printf( "*POLYLINETO*\n" );
04298       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
04299 #if 0
04300       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
04301       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
04302       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
04303         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
04304 #else
04305       edit_pointlarray( "\t", cptl, lpoints );
04306 #endif
04307     }
04308 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
04309   };

04352       apts[0].y = 0;

04357

04394       return true;
04395     }

04406     {
04407       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
04408       PolylineTo16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
04409     }
04410 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
04411 
04414     void edit ( void ) const
04415     {
04416       printf( "*POLYLINETO16*\n" );
04417       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
04418       edit_point16array( "\t", cpts, lpoints );
04419     }
04420 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
